div.p: David Cross Page

text.p: David Cross
Web: http://davecross.ca
Email: davidcross5000@gmail.com

div.p: Programmer/Analyst

text.p: I am a Self-motivated Programmer/Analyst with a proven ability to complete complex and challenging assignments under pressure, in a team-based environment. I excel in research, analysis and problem solving. One of my accomplishments was the development of a financial application that balanced 7 billion dollars of assets to within 3 cents.

div.p: Technical Expertise

text.p: Languages: Visual Basic 6, VB.NET, Word, Excel and Access VBA, PHP, MPower, C#.NET, ASP.NET, Progress 8/9 (GUI/CHUI), Javascript, ASP, HTML, WSH, DHTML, SQL, and UNIX.

Databases: SQL Server, Progress, Access, MySQL, XML, Oracle Platforms: Windows, DOS, UNIX (HP, Solaris), LINUX Applications: Dreamweaver, Visual SourceSafe, PVCS (Source Code Control), Photoshop, Office, SQL Navigator, XML Spy, LiveMotion, Fireworks, and FireStarter
Methodologies: Object-Oriented Software Engineering, Rapid Application Development, SDLC

div.p: EMPLOYMENT HISTORY
ADP Payroll Systems – Brampton, ON DEC 2007 – PRESENT
CONTRACT PROGRAMMER/ANALYST

text.p: Responsible for Development, and Programming.

div.p: Developing and Maintaining Interfaces between ADP and Clients using VB.NET
Sodexho Services – Food and Custodial Services DEC 2006 – DEC 2007
PROGRAMMER/ANALYST

text.p: Responsible for Development, and Programming.

div.p: Developed and Maintained 3 Tier Payroll Application using VB.NET, VBA, SQL Server
Developed Scanning Application using VB.NET and Windows Image Aquistition APIs
World Vision Canada – Christian Relief Organization MAR 2006 – OCT 2006
CONTRACT PROGRAMMER/ANALYST

text.p: Responsible for Development, and Programming.

div.p: Developed Call Center Applicatiion using VB.NET.
Developed Automated File Encryption and Compression application using VB.NET, and PGP.
Toronto Dominion Bank – Wealth Management, Toronto Feb 2005 – Mar 2006
CONTRACT PROGRAMMER/ANALYST

text.p: Responsible for Development, and Programming.

div.p: Designed and developed a myriad of VB 6, and VBA applications, specific to Daily Trades of Mutual Funds.
Developed AS400/Visual Basic Interfaces for Mutual Fund application using Attachmate Extra and VBA.
Developed Website using PHP and MySQL.
Developed Mpower/Cognos 4GL language applications for Mutual Fund Trading.
Berkshire Securities Corporation Canada, Burlington Sept 2002 – Feb 2005

text.p: Berkshire/AIC is the largest privately held Mutual Fund Company in Canada.

div.p: PROGRAMMER/ANALYST

text.p: Responsible for programming, administration and technical support.

div.p: Designed and developed Tools to facilitate overnight batch process using VB.NET & XML.
Developed and Designed Bug Tracking Web App using PHP and MySQL
Developed and Designed Performance Statistical application for use by Network Administrators.
Invensys Corporation Canada, Burlington May 2000 – Nov 2001

text.p: A leading Maintenance Management software developer.

div.p: VISUAL BASIC 6 DEVELOPER

text.p: Responsible for programming, development, training, customer service and administration.

div.p: Maintained and developed Visual Basic interface for AS400 using VB 6 and VBA.
Developed enhancements to Imaging Product using Visual Basic 6 and AS400 API’s.
Created database and Multimedia applications for Marketing department using VB 6. Corporate office requested these applications be adopted throughout sister organizations worldwide.
Created and taught 2 training classes for software products.
Developed, maintained Visual WorkPlace, Imaging and Executive Information System software products and related install programs using VB 6.

text.p: Spectrum Mutual Funds, Toronto May 1999 – May 2000

div.p: PROGRESS PROGRAMMER ANALYST

text.p: Responsible for development, programming, and analysis.

div.p: Developed tax reconciliation program that balanced yearly withholding tax with assets of 7 billion dollars to within .03 cents using Progress Char 8.1
Developed Applications, Interfaces and Reports using Progress Char 8.1.
DST Canada, Toronto February-May 1999
PROGRESS PROGRAMMER ANALYST

text.p: A mutual fund software developer for Morgan Stanley, BPI, Scotia McLeod, CIBC and others.

div.p: Enhanced mutual fund dilution module for emerging Japanese mutual fund market (Progress 8.1).
Developed Mutual Fund Software using Progress Char 8.1.
Completed a comprehensive Mutual Fund Training course.
EDUCATION

text.p: Diploma, Computer Programming, Sheridan College, Oakville (1996-1998)

The program focused on relational database design and implementation, SQL, data communication, Rapid Application Development Programming using Visual Basic, Business Mathematics, UNIX Programming and Systems Analysis.

div.p: Received Certificate with High Honors and as a Sheridan Scholar
Awarded the Silver Medal by Sheridan Board of Governors for highest G.P.A. in the Program (3.85).
Completed Sun Solaris UNIX Course 2001
Completed Advanced Visual Basic Course 2002
Completed Advanced VB.NET Course 2003
